title: Sentinel UI: Minimal Local Web Interface (DID info, VC summary, health dashboard, log viewer)
description: Implement a minimal local web UI for the sentinel, served at /ui, using FastAPI with Jinja2 templates and minimal static assets (no heavy frontend framework). The UI surfaces essential operational information: the sentinel's DID identity, active Verifiable Credentials (summary view), health and sync status, and recently logged events from TASK-051's ring buffer. The UI is intentionally minimal — no full VC payload display by default (expandable with warning), no mutation endpoints (use CLI for key rotation). Served only on localhost by default.
objectives: Implement /ui router in FastAPI with Jinja2 templates
Page /ui/did: DID string and Ed25519 public key fingerprint
Page /ui/credentials: list active VCs (type, exp, aud, env, statusListId) — no full payload by default
Page /ui/health: uptime, config bundle version/age, credential sync status, chain cache age, Δ staleness
Page /ui/logs: last 100 events from LogRingBuffer with PERMIT/DENY filter
Bind UI to 127.0.0.1 only by default (SENTINEL_UI_HOST env override)

steps.title: Create UI module structure
steps.detail: Create `sentinel/ui/` package. Register `ui_router = APIRouter(prefix='/ui', tags=['ui'])` and include in the app created by `create_app()` (TASK-037). Templates directory: `sentinel/ui/templates/` using Jinja2. Static files: `sentinel/ui/static/` with minimal CSS (no framework — use inline styles or tiny hand-written CSS). No JavaScript frameworks (React, Vue) — plain JS for expandable VC sections only. All pages return full HTML (not SPA). Mount static files: `app.mount('/ui/static', StaticFiles(directory='sentinel/ui/static'))`. Serve only if `SENTINEL_UI_ENABLED=true` (default true in local mode, false in K8s prod by default).

steps.title: Page /ui/did — Identity information
steps.detail: Route: `GET /ui/did`. Template: `did.html`. Context: `{did: str, key_fingerprint: str, service_id: str, env: str, instance_id: str, role: str}`. `key_fingerprint` = `sha256(public_key_bytes).hexdigest()[:16]` — NOT the public key itself (prevents key material exposure). Display: styled card with DID, fingerprint, service_id, env, role, instance_id, and uptime. Add a copy-to-clipboard button (vanilla JS, copy DID string). Note on page: 'This DID is your sentinel identity. Share it with Discovery administrators for enrollment.'

steps.title: Page /ui/credentials — VC summary
steps.detail: Route: `GET /ui/credentials`. Template: `credentials.html`. Fetch credentials from `CredentialStore` (TASK-038) — summary only: `{type, issuer_did_hash (8 chars), exp, aud, env, status_list_id, status: active|expired|revoked}`. Render as an HTML table with columns: Type, Expires, Audience, Env, Status. Do NOT render full JWT. Add expandable section (HTML `<details>` element, no JS required) showing: `{issuer_did_hash, jti_hash (8 chars), nbf}`. Show warning inside expanded section: 'Extended credential metadata. Do not share this page content externally.' Sort by exp ascending (soonest expiry first = most urgent to renew).

steps.title: Page /ui/health — Operational status
steps.detail: Route: `GET /ui/health` (NOT the same as API `/health/live` or `/health/ready`). Template: `health.html`. Gather data from app state: `{uptime_seconds, config_bundle_version, config_bundle_age_seconds, last_credential_sync_at, credential_count, chain_cache_age_seconds, revocation_delta_seconds, revocation_staleness_seconds, trust_layer_status: ok|stale|unavailable, policy_version, policy_rule_count, instance_id, sentinel_did_hash}`. Color-code status indicators: green (all ok), yellow (stale > Δ/2), red (unavailable or stale > Δ). Refresh the page every 30s via `<meta http-equiv='refresh' content='30'>`.

steps.title: Page /ui/logs — Recent events
steps.detail: Route: `GET /ui/logs?decision=&service_id=`. Template: `logs.html`. Fetch last 100 events from `LogRingBuffer.get_recent(n=100, filter_decision=request.query_params.get('decision'), filter_service_id=request.query_params.get('service_id'))`. Render as HTML table: ts, direction, decision (colored: PERMIT=green, DENY=red), error_code, service_id, latency_ms, jti_hash (8 chars). Add filter form at top: dropdowns for decision (All/PERMIT/DENY) and service_id. Include link to live stream: 'View live log stream →' pointing to TASK-055 endpoint. Log entries are pre-redacted by SentinelLogger — no further redaction needed at display layer.

steps.title: Navigation and layout template
steps.detail: Create `base.html` Jinja2 template with: (1) Top navigation bar with links: DID, Credentials, Health, Logs, Live Stream. (2) Breadcrumb showing current page. (3) Footer: 'Sentinel UI — internal only. Do not expose to public networks.'. (4) CSP header via middleware: `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:`. The `'unsafe-inline'` for script is acceptable given this is a localhost-only page — do not add complex nonce-based CSP for this minimal UI.

steps.title: Bind to localhost by default
steps.detail: In `sentinel/core/config.py`: `SENTINEL_UI_HOST = os.getenv('SENTINEL_UI_HOST', '127.0.0.1')`. Pass to uvicorn via: `uvicorn.run(app, host=SENTINEL_UI_HOST, port=SENTINEL_PORT)`. If `SENTINEL_UI_HOST` is set to `0.0.0.0` in config: log WARNING at startup: `event=ui_exposed_on_all_interfaces host=0.0.0.0. Ensure network access control is in place.`. For Kubernetes: UI is served on the pod's loopback only; use `kubectl port-forward` for access. Document in TASK-049 runbook.

steps.title: Tests
steps.detail: Test file: `tests/unit/ui/test_ui_pages.py`. Using `httpx.AsyncClient(app=app, base_url='http://test')` (ASGI test client). Cases: (1) GET /ui/did returns 200 HTML with DID string; (2) GET /ui/credentials returns table without full JWT content; (3) GET /ui/health returns 200 with health fields; (4) GET /ui/logs returns last 100 events; (5) filter GET /ui/logs?decision=deny returns only deny events; (6) no private key bytes appear in any page response; (7) no Authorization header values appear in any page response. Assert rendered HTML with BeautifulSoup.

files.tests/unit/ui/test_ui_pages.py: UI page tests with ASGI test client

security_constraints: UI binds to 127.0.0.1 by default — exposing to 0.0.0.0 requires explicit env var with WARNING log
No full JWT or VC payload rendered in default view (only in hidden expand section with warning)
CSP headers prevent external script injection — default-src 'self'
No mutation endpoints in UI — key rotation, enrollment, and revocation only via sentinelctl CLI
DID displayed in full (needed for identity verification) but private key material never displayed
X-Frame-Options: DENY header set to prevent clickjacking on UI pages
acceptance_criteria: All 4 UI pages render without errors with populated mock sentinel state
No private key or raw JWT appears in any rendered HTML response
Log filter by decision correctly returns only matching events
Health page shows correct color-coding based on staleness thresholds
UI is not accessible from 0.0.0.0 without explicit env override
CSP and X-Frame-Options headers present on all UI responses
